target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public entity (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. While 11 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, filtering for stage-appropriate comparables (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median-based triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and obscure meaningful comparisons. The fragmented stage distribution and wide valuation dispersion across the robotics sector—ranging from early-stage private companies to mature public operators—makes narrow-set comparability analysis unreliable at this early stage.

Without sufficient peer density at seed stage to support quantitative triangulation, valuation support relies on qualitative assessment: RoboTwin's €2.3M raise positions it at the lower end of institutional seed activity, and follow-on valuation should reflect typical seed-to-Series A markdown patterns in deep-tech robotics (typically 1.5x–3x current round valuation). The key uncertainty remains the absence of meaningful stage-cohort comparables; as the company progresses toward Series A, denser Series A peer sets should enable more robust benchmarking.
